architecture: MVVM + Clean layers: UI (Compose/View) -> ViewModel -> UseCase -> Repository -> data source.
Dependency injection with Hilt; scope dependencies correctly.
Expose UI state from the ViewModel as StateFlow; collect with lifecycle awareness.
Domain models separate from network DTOs and DB entities; map at the repository.
conventions: All async via coroutines/Flow; never block the main thread; no GlobalScope — use viewModelScope.
Represent UI state with a sealed class/interface (Loading/Success/Error).
Immutable data classes; use when-expressions and Kotlin idioms (let/apply/require).
Propagate errors as Result or sealed state; do not swallow exceptions.
UI strings in resources (strings.xml), not hardcoded.
lint: Run ktlint/detekt and Android Lint; fix issues in changed scope.
No !! non-null assertions in production paths; handle nullability explicitly.
Run unit tests (JUnit) for ViewModel/UseCase logic.
templates: scaffold-data: Retrofit API interface + DTO (nullable-safe) + Repository interface/impl + mapper to domain model.
scaffold-screen: Composable screen + ViewModel exposing StateFlow<UiState> + navigation route registration.
Mirror the module structure the app already uses; do not invent a new one.
design-system: Use Material 3 via Compose: MaterialTheme.colorScheme / typography / shapes — never hardcode colors.
Size with dp/sp; reuse shared composables before creating new ones.
Support light/dark theme and dynamic color where enabled.
Handle insets/safe areas; content descriptions for accessibility.
library-docs: Networking: Retrofit + OkHttp + a JSON converter (Moshi/kotlinx.serialization) — match the repo.
Async: Coroutines + Flow. Local DB: Room. DI: Hilt.
UI: Jetpack Compose + Navigation Compose; follow existing usage, don't mix Views/Compose arbitrarily.
